zh.lifeSummary: 當William Seeley於大約1513年出生在英國亨廷格多史爾格亞特 格藍斯登時，他的父親Thomas Seeley24歲，母親Dorothy25歲。他於1538年與Elizabeth Burr在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾格亞特 格藍斯登結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1565年12月8日在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾格亞特 格藍斯登享年53歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾格亞特 格藍斯登。

nameMeanings.name: Seeley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a person with a cheerful disposition, from Middle English seli ‘happy, fortunate’ (Old English sǣlig, from sǣl ‘happiness, good fortune’). The word was also occasionally used as a female personal name during the Middle Ages. The sense ‘pitiable’, which developed into modern English silly, is not attested before the 15th century. See also Selman .
Altered form of German   Seele  , respelled to preserve the bisyllabic pronunciation of the German name.

variants.name: Selley
meanings: habitational name from Selly Oak in Northfield (Worcestershire), probably from Old English scelf ‘shelf, ledge’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

variants.name: Selman
meanings: English: nickname for a happy or fortunate man, from Middle English seli ‘happy, fortunate’ (Old English sǣlig) + man. The nickname was also used as a personal name. See also Seeley .
English: from the Middle English nickname or female personal name Sely (see Seeley ) + -man, hence an occupational name for a servant of someone so called.

variants.name: Sealy
meanings: Welsh: from the male personal name Selyf or Seleu, medieval Welsh vernacular forms of Solomon which were replaced by that form in the Welsh Bible of 1588.
Irish: variant of   Shelley   (in counties Cork and Kerry); elsewhere of English or Welsh origin, as in 1 above and 2.

variants.name: Seley
meanings: English: variant of   Seeley  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When William Seeley was born about 1513, in Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, England, his father, Thomas Seeley, was 24 and his mother, Dorothy, was 25. He married Elizabeth Burr in 1538, in Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 8 December 1565, in Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53, and was buried in Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Seeley est né environ 1513, à Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Seeley, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Dorothy, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Burr en 1538, à Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 8 décembre 1565, à Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Great Gransden, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Seeleyが約1513年にイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアグレアト グランスデンで生まれた時，父Thomas Seeleyは24歳，母Dorothyは25歳であった。1538年にイギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアグレアト グランスデンでElizabeth Burrと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1565年12月8日にイギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアグレアト グランスデンで53歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアグレアト グランスデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Seeley(이)가 대략 1513년 영국 헌틴그도실르 그리트 그랜즈든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Seeley(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Dorothy(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1538년 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 그리트 그랜즈든에서 Elizabeth Burr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1565년 12월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 그리트 그랜즈든에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 그리트 그랜즈든에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Seeley родился около 1513 г. в местности грит грансден, хантингдоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Seeley было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Dorothy, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Burr в 1538 г. в местности грит грансден, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 8 декабря 1565 г. в местности грит грансден, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности грит грансден, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания.
